title: Flywheel Profile — per-repo declarative flywheel contract
description: Build a per-repo .flywheel/profile (flat shell, KEY=value) that is the single declarative contract for a repo's flywheel behavior, read ONLY at the edges (kickoff generator, ship bead, issue-projection) and NEVER by the core br/bv/Agent-Mail loop. ABSENCE of the file = pure flywheel/Emmanuel defaults (solo, no PR, no projection, light gates, no worktrees). Full plan: docs/specs/flywheel-profile/PLAN.md. Format chosen by user = flat shell (no TOML/Python). Minimal+accretive; fw stays link/setup/list. Reconciles with skills-ogi.8/.9/.10 (see U2/U5).

title: U1 Resolver: flywheel-profile.sh (parse + Emmanuel defaults + pm detection)
description: Plan §5/§16. New file scripts/flywheel-profile.sh (+ tests). Bash, run FROM the skill: 'flywheel-profile.sh --repo DIR' emits eval-able FLYWHEEL_* vars on stdout. Logic: (1) seed Emmanuel defaults MODE=solo WORKTREES=false PRECOMMIT=light PREPUSH=full PROJECTION_APP=''; (2) if .flywheel/profile exists, for each known key grep -E '^FLYWHEEL_X=' + VALIDATE value against its allowed set (else warn-to-stderr + keep default) — do NOT 'source' the file (injection guard); (3) detect package manager -> FLYWHEEL_PM: package.json packageManager field first, else lockfile (pnpm-lock.yaml->pnpm, bun.lockb->bun, package-lock.json->npm, yarn.lock->yarn), else none; (4) outside a git repo / no package file -> defaults + PM=none. Read-only, network-free. ACCEPTANCE (§18): emits correct FLYWHEEL_* for present/absent/invalid/partial profiles + every pm case; tests green; never sources repo content. Touches ONLY scripts/flywheel-profile.sh + its tests.
close_reason: Implemented flywheel-profile.sh resolver with tests

title: Lease guard: chain into .git/hooks/pre-commit on non-husky repos
description: On a NON-husky repo, ntm init installs .git/hooks/pre-commit (beads sync), then install_guard's 'ntm guards install' fallback FAILS (hook already exists at .git/hooks/pre-commit). Fix scripts/flywheel-link.sh install_guard: for a non-husky repo with an existing .git/hooks/pre-commit, CHAIN the portable file-reservation-guard.sh into it (copy guard to scripts/ci/ + prepend a call), mirroring the husky branch, instead of ntm guards install. Found setting up the skills repo for the skills-bg7 swarm. Touches scripts/flywheel-link.sh (RESERVE via Agent Mail — skills-ogi.8 + U2 also edit it). ACCEPTANCE: non-husky repo setup chains the guard with no error; both the beads-sync hook and the lease guard run on commit.
close_reason: Chained portable lease guard into existing non-husky git pre-commit hooks

title: Cheatsheet: reliable Claude supervisor — spawn via --cc from START, demote ntm controller
description: DIAGNOSIS (diagnosing-bugs loop): the Claude controller keeps failing because 'ntm controller' RETRO-FITS Claude onto pane 1 of an already-running session; when pane 1 is a busy Codex worker, the retitle/respawn does NOT cleanly launch Claude (pane retitled controller_claude_1 but still running Codex). RULED OUT via probe loop: stale model (claude --model claude-opus-4-6 -p ok returns OK) and auth (the 401 was a transient session expiry; claude -p ok returns OK now). VERIFIED FIX: ntm spawn <name> --cc=1 --cod=N (or a cc-inclusive recipe: review-team / balanced) launches Claude in its OWN fresh pane reliably — a throwaway --cc=1 spawn showed a working Claude Code UI. This matches Emmanuel: the supervisor is part of the swarm composition, not bolted on after. ACTION: update references/cheatsheet.md §3 controller section — (1) primary/reliable path = spawn the supervisor via --cc from the START (or a cc recipe); (2) demote 'ntm controller' to 'unreliable on a busy session'; (3) keep existing notes: still parks at bypass-permissions welcome (accept it), feed via tmux send-keys not ntm send, ensure claude authed (preflight probe). Coordinate via Agent Mail with U6. ACCEPTANCE: cheatsheet recommends --cc-from-start as primary, ntm controller marked unreliable.
close_reason: Updated cheatsheet to recommend spawning Claude supervisor with --cc=1 from the start and demote ntm controller to recovery-only

title: U3 Kickoff generator: flywheel-kickoff.sh (full launch recipe)
description: Plan §7 (runner-up 1). New file scripts/flywheel-kickoff.sh (+ tests), run FROM the skill: 'flywheel-kickoff.sh <session> --plan <path> [--cod N=3] [--cass <area>]'. eval the resolver (eval "$(flywheel-profile.sh --repo .)") — NO jq. Emit the FULL launch recipe to stdout: (1) single-line 'ntm spawn .. --init-prompt ..' with pm-correct commands + mode-aware tail (team->final ship bead opens PR ready+merge when green; solo->commit to working branch, no PR) + ALWAYS one shared tree/NEVER worktrees + if PRECOMMIT=heavy add 'do hook-align first'; (2) controller recipe: ntm controller <session> then tmux send-keys -t <session>:0.<pane> '<brief>' + Enter (NOT ntm send -> mangles to [Image]); (3) readiness note: if 0/N ready, respawn cod + re-send kickoff + coordinator assign. Shell-quote every profile value; init-prompt = one quoted arg (injection guard). Does NOT spawn itself. ACCEPTANCE: single-line correctly-quoted mode+pm-aware spawn + controller recipe + readiness note; snapshot tests for solo/team x pnpm/bun. Touches ONLY scripts/flywheel-kickoff.sh + tests.

title: U5 Issue-projection app=linear, compute/apply split (continues skills-ogi.10)
description: Plan §9/§10. CONTINUES + COMPLETES skills-ogi.10 (portable opt-in Linear projection). Make the beads-linear-projector a portable SKILL ASSET (run from the skill with --repo) that does COMPUTE only: read beads + .flywheel/projects.tsv (epic<TAB>project-id; the single source, replacing per-epic linear_project_id metadata) -> emit deterministic linearMcp ops as JSON. NO MCP needed for compute. APPLY is a SEPARATE step needing the tracker MCP -> run by the human / Claude controller, NEVER the Codex workers (non-interactive Codex lacks the interactive Linear MCP). TRIGGER (MVP): projection runs as a final sync in the ship bead by the MCP-holder, not a per-close loop step (decoupled). FAILURE (locked): PROJECTION_APP set but tracker not connected/authed, or unknown app -> log visible error + CONTINUE; empty/absent -> silent no-op. ACCEPTANCE: COMPUTE maps epic->project from projects.tsv + emits ops; error-and-continues when tracker unavailable; APPLY documented as MCP-holder step. Touches the projector asset + projects.tsv handling.
close_reason: Added compute-only beads-linear-projector asset reading .flywheel/projects.tsv and .beads/issues.jsonl to emit deterministic Linear MCP operation JSON, with silent no-op when projection is unset and visible continue-on-error payloads for missing/unsupported projection config. title: Fix conductor-poll epic-scoping: count slug-id children (reality-check finding)
description:
conductor-poll.sh under-reports epic progress when children have slug-based ids.

REPRO (from live certify run 2026-07-02): certify created an epic with 3 children via `br create --parent <epic> --slug cert-one ...`. beads assigned the children non-dotted ids (e.g. `<session>-cert-one-xxx`), NOT `<epic>.N`. In conductor-poll.sh the python `in_scope()` matches children with `rid(r).startswith(epic + ".")`, so slug-id children were excluded from `children`; progress.closed stayed 0/3 forever even though the worker had created all files and closed every bead + the parent epic. Consequence: conductor-certify.sh never saw closed>=1, so it NEVER injected the pane-kill, so G7 recovery was never exercised and the run hit TIMEOUT (exit 1) despite the workflow actually succeeding.

FIX: scope children to the epic by the beads parent/epic relationship (the `parent`/`epic_id`/`parent_id` field on each row) instead of (or in addition to) dotted-id prefix. Handle both dotted-id children (`<epic>.N`, as in skills-flywheel-conductor-nme.*) AND slug-id children. Do NOT special-case formats with brittle string ops — key off the actual parent field the beads JSON exposes (inspect `br list --json` / `br show <epic> --json` to confirm the field name).

HARD DELIVERABLE (deterministic): add a regression case to tests/conductor-poll.test.sh with a mocked `br` returning an epic + slug-id children where some are closed; assert progress.closed counts them. Keep existing dotted-id cases green.

Also verify conductor-certify.sh injected-kill path fires once closed>=1 (its `closed>=1` guard depends on this fix). Adjust certify only if the fix surfaces a second assumption there.

Files: skills/engineering/flywheel-conductor/scripts/conductor-poll.sh, skills/engineering/flywheel-conductor/tests/conductor-poll.test.sh (and conductor-certify.sh only if needed). Reserve these via Agent Mail before editing. This BLOCKS the reality-check (.12) and therefore ship (.13).
design:
SECOND defect in the same file (found in live dogfood 2026-07-02) — fix together, one worker, one reservation on conductor-poll.sh:

conductor-poll.sh pane classification misdetects dead panes. When a codex pane self-updates it prints "Please restart Codex" / "Update ran successfully" and drops to a shell prompt like:
  rgbrgy:skills/ (feat/flywheel-conductor*) $                 [20:55:57]
The shell regex re.search(r"(\$|%)\s*$", tail_txt) fails because the line ends with a bracketed timestamp + padding AFTER the prompt char, so the pane is misclassified "idle" instead of "shell"; triage then never raises G7 and dead workers go unrecovered.

FIX: (a) detect shell prompts even with trailing timestamp/padding after the $/% prompt char; (b) recognize the restart/update banner as a distinct dead/needs-restart pane state that conductor-triage.sh escalates to G7. Add deterministic regression fixtures + assertions to tests/conductor-poll.test.sh (and evals/ if used) for BOTH this and the slug-id scoping bug. Keep all existing cases green.
close_reason: Fixed conductor-poll epic scoping for slug-id children via parent/dependents metadata and added dead-pane restart detection regressions.

title: U9 conductor-certify.sh — live self-test
description: Plan §3.3+3.6. Sandbox repo in $TMPDIR: git init, link via launcher, br init, 3-bead env-free docs epic, spawn --cod=1 via kickoff, drive via poll/triage to green, INJECT one pane-kill mid-run and assert detect+respawn within one cycle, teardown, write .flywheel/runtime/certified.json in TARGET repo. --repo flag; exit 0 = certified. Costs real tokens: print estimate + require --yes. Deps U1,U6. Acceptance: script runs end-to-end on this machine (live run is the RC bead).
close_reason: certify harness: sandbox epic, one worker, injected pane-kill w/ G7 detect+respawn assertion, certified.json + journal line; syntax + refusal checks green; LIVE run is the reality-check bead

title: Harden flywheel-local-launcher skill from dogfood lessons
description: Build the 10 dogfood lessons from the platform-monorepo de-bp run into the skill. Cheatsheet is HUMAN-facing (keep day-to-day only). Move setup/config to a dedicated references/setup.md. Review from BOTH perspectives: a new workstream on an existing flywheel repo, AND a brand-new project with no flywheel setup. Be precise + concise; remove repetition.
close_reason: All child beads closed; focused launcher shell checks are green

title: S1 create references/setup.md (consolidate install + per-machine + per-repo + new-project/symlink-consistency fix + bv tutorial + xhigh->high config + agent-mail server)

title: Make beads->Linear projection portable + opt-in for ALL flywheel repos
description: DECISION LOCKED (user): skill-asset approach — carry the projector in the skill (like the lease guard) + a small per-repo config for the epic<->Linear-project mapping, aligned to how flywheel tooling configures itself (.ntm / .ubsignore / .beads pattern). Build: (1) port beads-linear-projector to a portable skill asset runnable in node + bun repos; (2) add a per-repo opt-in config (e.g. .flywheel/linear.json or epic linear_project_id metadata) that fw setup can scaffold; (3) document in setup.md — beads = source of truth, a Linear Project is a ONE-WAY mirror of one epic, deterministic linearMcp ops applied via the Linear MCP, NEVER in the core br/bv/Agent-Mail loop; (4) wire opt-in for platform-monorepo + customer-template. ACCEPTANCE: projector runs from any flywheel repo via its per-repo config; setup.md documents opt-in + determinism + decoupling; both repos can map an epic when desired.
close_reason: Continued as skills-bg7 U5 (skills-bg7.5): portable opt-in projection is now the issue-projection app=linear unit (compute/apply split, projects.tsv). See Flywheel Profile epic.

title: S6 husky-aware lease guard + preflight curl timeout (found verifying skill on customer-template)
close_reason: setup chains the portable lease guard into existing husky pre-commit instead of ntm-guards-install (which fails on husky repos); preflight curl now has --max-time 3 so it can't hang. Verified end-to-end on customer-template.

title: S7 fold swarm-launch lessons into cheatsheet
close_reason: Added to cheatsheet §3: pre-spawn codex/claude current+authed check (stale Codex self-updates and drops panes to shell); 0/N-ready recovery (respawn cod + re-send kickoff + assign); CC controller flakiness (401/login, bypass screen, and feed brief via tmux send-keys NOT ntm send which pastes as [Image]); ntm kill --force.

title: preflight: probe codex/claude auth + currency (not just installed)
description: flywheel-link.sh preflight checks tools are INSTALLED but not AUTHED/CURRENT. A stale/logged-out Codex passes preflight, then self-updates or 401s on first spawn and drops panes to the shell (hit on customer-template). Add a NON-FATAL warning: probe codex (echo ok | codex exec returns text; flag if it just updated) and claude login. Keep it a warning so it stays modular. ACCEPTANCE: preflight flags an unauthed/stale codex or claude before spawn; never hard-fails on it.
close_reason: Added non-fatal bounded Codex/Claude preflight probes that warn on unauthenticated sessions and Codex restart/update signals; verified with bash -n, real preflight skip path, and stubbed probe scenarios. ShellCheck unavailable; UBS staged exits 0 but has no bash scanner.

title: setup: detect + warn on a heavy pre-commit (commit-immediately crawls)
description: A repo whose pre-commit runs typecheck/build/test on EVERY commit makes commit-immediately crawl/stall (customer-template needed a bead to split fast-pre-commit / heavy-pre-push). setup should DETECT (grep .husky/pre-commit for typecheck|build|test) and WARN with the suggested split, WITHOUT forcing it — not every repo has husky; stay modular. ACCEPTANCE: setup warns when pre-commit looks heavy; forces nothing.
close_reason: Superseded by skills-bg7 U2 (skills-bg7.2): the [gates] pre_commit detection + scaffold warning IS the heavy-pre-commit warn. See Flywheel Profile epic.
